mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.41% as of 2023-07-15 suggests accommodative stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk and equity risk premium, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2023-07-15 remains near historical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS deflated by CPIAUCSL) above the 3-month moving average indicates robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions flow through equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between real sales growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive sales surprises fail to predict positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side contraction that compresses profit margins and reduces forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side shock or post-peak manufacturing cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO declines > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both accelerate simultaneously, indicating synchronized demand expansion across residential and labor markets that signals broad-based economic strength and positive equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 3-month MA AND PAYEMS monthly change > 150k
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1192k and PAYEMS at 147810k as of 2023-07-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with labor market strength
failure_condition: Rank IC for joint HOUST-PAYEMS acceleration signal falls below 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive joint signals fail to predict positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year) remains above 3%, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy that constrains equity valuations through elevated discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.41% and CPIAUCSL at 249.245 as of 2023-07-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and inflation > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50k monthly additions, signaling labor market weakness that precedes earnings downgrades and increased recession probability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 50k for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 68% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 50k monthly, or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while unemployment remains below 4.5%, indicating a late-cycle squeeze where growth slows but labor remains tight, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets reprice terminal growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17272.468B and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2023-07-15
failure_condition: Rank IC for the joint GDPC1-UNRATE signal falls below 0.032 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in late-cycle squeeze periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
